# Everything That Matters, Only What Matters

**Flexibility: Expected (SHOULD)**

## The Commitment

Governance captures every piece of information needed to operate correctly,
and nothing more. Missing information creates gaps. Unnecessary information
creates noise. Noise is as dangerous as gaps — it dilutes attention and hides
what matters.

## What This Means

- Every governed component includes what a consumer needs to use it correctly.
- Every field in a governance schema justifies its existence with a consumer need.
- Unused fields are removed, not left empty.
- "Nice to have" information is explicitly optional, never required.

## Violation Looks Like

A governance template with 30 required fields where only 8 are used. A document
missing the one piece of information a consumer actually needs. A registry
cluttered with deprecated entries that nobody removed.

## Derived From

- JOS-C02 (Requisite Parsimony) — minimum viable complexity
- JOS-C07 (Knowledge Decay) — noise accelerates decay by diluting signal
